pages.text: "Leo, please put on your shoes," said Dad. Leo nodded. He was trying his best.
Leo reached for his shoes. Then Pip noticed something on the floor — a green brick.
"ZO:OOMIE!" whispered Pip. Leo's brain lit up. The brick looked very interesting indeed.
Soon Leo was building a spaceship. Then playing with Marmalade the cat. Then camping under a star blanket.
To Leo and Pip, it felt like thirty seconds. One wonderful thing led to another.
Dad came back. "Ten minutes have passed," he said gently. Leo's shoes were still waiting.
"Your brain isn't being naughty," Dad said. "It just gets excited by interesting things." Pip looked relieved.
Pip popped something into the pocket — a Pause Pebble. "A little reminder," Pip smiled. "Will this still matter after I've finished my shoes?"
